items.question: You engineered `sessions_per_week` (0–12) and `revenue_per_session` ($5–$500). Which pipeline change is required before k-NN?
options: Add StandardScaler on the numeric branch after imputation
items.answer: Add StandardScaler on the numeric branch after imputation
items.explanation: Engineered columns can still have very different ranges; k-NN uses distance and needs a scaler branch. See scaling and normalization for StandardScaler details.

items.question: A HistGradientBoostingClassifier uses engineered `txn_count` and `amount_ratio`. Should the numeric ColumnTransformer branch include StandardScaler?
options: No—tree splits are scale-invariant on monotone transforms
items.answer: No—tree splits are scale-invariant on monotone transforms
items.explanation: Pure tree/boosting models on engineered numerics usually omit the scaler branch; algorithm sensitivity drives the decision.

items.question: Where should StandardScaler run in a typical FE workflow for Ridge on engineered numerics?
options: After imputation on the numeric branch, before Ridge
Before feature engineering on raw columns only
items.answer: After imputation on the numeric branch, before Ridge
items.explanation: Scale continuous engineered features after impute/encode, immediately before regularized linear estimators so penalties apply fairly.

items.question: Why should one-hot columns usually skip StandardScaler in ColumnTransformer?
options: Scaling distorts 0/1 indicator semantics without helping distance or penalties
items.answer: Scaling distorts 0/1 indicator semantics without helping distance or penalties
items.explanation: Keep encodings in the categorical branch; apply scalers only to continuous engineered numerics.

items.question: Canonical StandardScaler and MinMaxScaler formulas are defined in which topic?
items.explanation: The preprocessing topic owns scaler math and APIs; the FE topic links there and owns pipeline placement after engineered features.
